input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>A hand reaches from the left towards a clear plastic bag holding white wired earphones on a light wooden table. The fingers touch and press against the packaging, interacting with the coiled white cable inside. White Russian text remains visible at the bottom of the screen.</think><action>silent</action>

compressed_segments.text: [0-10] A hand reaches from the left to touch a clear plastic bag holding white wired earphones on a light wooden table. Fingers press against the packaging, then grasp and crumple the plastic edge, manipulating the coiled white cable within. Throughout this interaction, the white Russian text "ДЕЛАЕМ САМИ ПРОСТОЙ МИКРОФОН-ПЕТЛИЧКУ" remains at the bottom. The hand tightens its grip, compressing the earphones, before repositioning to hold the left edge, flattening the bag to reveal the black-ringed audio jack through the transparent material. [10-14] The white wired earphones are now removed from the packaging, resting on the table. A hand grasps one white earbud, lifting the thin cable which is now untangled. The black-ringed 3.5mm audio jack is visible near the center. Finally, the hand lifts a loop of the white cable high above the surface, straightening it while the earbuds and jack remain on the wood.

[14-20] A hand manipulates the white inline remote featuring a black circle and an earbud of white wired earphones resting on a light wooden table. [20-26] A red tool with black handles cuts the white cable near the remote, displaying the watermark "www.youtube.com/user/shematikru". The action severs the cable, separating the earbud segment from the audio jack section. [26-28] A hand then grasps the white inline remote on the remaining cable segment, lifting it slightly while the detached earbuds lie separately on the right.

From [28-32], a hand holds a white inline remote, rotating it to reveal a small side hole before placing it flat on a light wooden table. Between [32-38], the hand grips the remote horizontally, maintaining the flat white cable looped around the device's body while a detached earbud segment rests undisturbed in the bottom right corner. Finally, from [38-42], the hand withdraws completely, leaving the remote resting on the table with the cable unwrapped and extending toward the audio jack and earbuds, all beneath the persistent white watermark www.youtube.com/user/shematikru.

[42-58] A white inline remote control featuring a shiny circular silver button and a white flat cable lies on a light wooden table. A hand grasps the device, rotating it to display the side seam of the plastic casing, then releases it. A silver metal craft knife with a knurled grip is introduced, and its blade is positioned against the remote's side seam. The knife is inserted into the seam to pry open the plastic housing, wedging the blade to separate the casing. Eventually, the white plastic casing is removed, revealing the internal components attached to the cable: a small black switch, a silver cylindrical microphone, and thin red and black wires resting on the table.

recent_thinks: [58-60] A hand enters from the left, grasping the white cable strain relief near the exposed internal components. The small silver microphone, black switch, and thin wires remain visible, resting on the light wooden table surface as the fingers hold the assembly.

[60-62] A hand continues to grasp the white cable strain relief near the exposed internal electronics resting on the light wooden surface. The small cylindrical silver microphone, black switch component, and thin connecting wires remain clearly visible and largely stationary as the fingers adjust their hold slightly on the assembly.

[62-64] A pair of pliers with silver jaws and red-black handles enters the frame from the right. The tool grips the thin metal legs of the small black switch component held by the left hand. The pliers twist and cut the legs, working to detach the switch from the white cable assembly resting on the light wooden table surface.

[64-66] Pliers with red and black handles grip and pull a metal leg from the small black switch component held by a hand. The tool severs the connection, separating part of the switch assembly from the thin wires of the white cable resting on the light wooden surface.
